fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Delaware Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Delaware - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity delaware, how to transfer physical therapist license to delaware, physical therapist compact delaware, delaware physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Delaware Board of Physical Therapy
board.mailing_address: Cannon Building, 861 Silver Lake Blvd., Suite 203, Dover, DE 19904

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years by March 31 of even-numbered years

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Delaware.

steps.title: Create a DELPROS Account
steps.description: Register as a new user on the DELPROS online portal at delpros.delaware.gov to initiate your application process.

steps.title: Complete Online Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Log into your DELPROS account, select the 'Physical Therapist' application type, complete all sections of the application, and pay the non-refundable application fee.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts, verifying completion of your accredited physical therapy program, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Delaware Board of Physical Therapy via DELPROS.

steps.title: Provide Verification of Licensure (for Endorsement)
steps.description: If you hold or have held a PT license in another U.S. state or territory, request official verification of licensure from each jurisdiction, sent directly to DELPROS. This verification must include your NPTE scores.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided within DELPROS after submitting your application to complete both state and federal criminal background checks through approved vendors.

steps.title: Review by the Board
steps.description: Once all required documents and fees are received and processed by DELPROS, your complete application will be reviewed by the Delaware Board of Physical Therapy for final approval.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Delaware physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Delaware have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Delaware participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Delaware without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Delaware?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Delaware while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Delaware
hero.dek: Physical Therapists seeking licensure in Delaware must submit their application and all required supporting documentation through the state's DELPROS online system.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: To create an account, navigate to the DELPROS login page and click 'Register a New User'. Follow the prompts to set up your account and then log in to start your application.
